item.title: Transportation — Public Comment on Contract Awards: Hardwood Timber for Fenders
item.description:
Outcome: Public Comment on Contract Awards
This is a Notice that the Department of Transportation is seeking comments from the public about the proposed contract below. Contract Type: Purchase Order/Contract Contractor: William G Moore & Son, Inc of Delaware Contractor Address: 218 Schanck Road, Freehold, NJ, 07728 Sope of Services: Hardwood Timber for Fenders Maximum Value: $500,000.00 Term: Notice to Proceed through August 4, 2028 E-Pin: 84126W0078001 Procurement Method: MWBE Non-Competitive Small Purchase Procurement Policy Board Rule: Rule 3-08(c)(1)(iv) How can I comment on this proposed contract award? Please submit your comment

item.title: Youth and Community Development — Procurement: Special Initiatives Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP) Vulnerable Youth WLG
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP) providers through a variety of program models, serves youth ages 14-24 throughout New York City by providing opportunities to become familiar with the world of work, gain employment experience, and identify educational pathways that support career and life goals. This program is integral to the City s workforce development strategy and provides a critical intervention point for underserved youth population.

item.title: Information Technology and Telecommunications — Public Comment on Contract Awards: 7-858-0823A - MWBE NYC311 UX / UI Designer - User Experience Designer (Lead)
item.description:
Outcome: Public Comment on Contract Awards
This notice from NYC Office of Technology and Innovation is to seek public comments on the proposed contract detailed below. Contract Type: CT1 Contractor: Abrahams Consulting, LLC. Contractor Address : P.O. Box 10266, Staten Island, NY 10301 Scope of Services: The UX UI Designer will be responsible for translating business requirements into User Experience Design deliverables for 311 CSMS because of enhancements and defect fixes among other things. Working with the 311 CSMS team, the UX UI Designer will be responsible for delivering interaction, visual, service and user experience design reco

Responsibilities include testing the highly available and flexible agency applications following industry best practices, compliance with OTI security standards, working collabor

item.title: Campaign Finance Board — Public Comment on Contract Awards: Payment Services
item.description:
Outcome: Public Comment on Contract Awards
This is a notice that the NYC Campaign Finance Board is seeking comments from the public about the proposed contract below: Contract Type: Contract (CT1) Contractor: Eleven3seven5 LLC Contractor Address: 32 Court Street, Brooklyn, NY 11201 Scope of Services: The Campaign Finance Board (CFB) is proposing a contract with Eleven3seven5 LLC to provide services covering individualized payments required to support the CFB s NYC Votes Youth Ambassador program and potential other programs or project. Maximum Value: $600,000.00 Term: 6/1/2024 through 5/31/2027 E-PIN: 004202400008A1 Procurement Method:

item.title: Small Business Services — Procurement: FY26 IBSP BOCNET - Queens/Bronx NAE
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
The NYC Department of Small Business Services is entering into a Negotiated Acquisition Extension Contract with Business Outreach Network Inc. to continue providing the technical consultant services for Industrial business zone businesses in Central Queens & the Bronx. This reinforces the City s efforts to support businesses of any size and at any stage, by responding to current and evolving needs while providing the conditions and resources to enable them to further grow and advance. The contract term will be from 7/1/2025 to 6/30/2026, in the amount of $340,000.00 The negotiated acquisition

item.title: Taxi and Limousine Commission — Special Materials: MRV RATIFICATION
item.description:
Outcome: Special Materials
On May 7, 2026, pursuant to Section 1-02(h)(1)(ii) of the Procurement Policy Board (PPB) Rules, the City Chief Procurement Officer ( CCPO ) ratified a minor PPB Rules violation request made by the New York City Taxi and Limousine Commission for an M/WBE Small Purchase Method procurement. TLC entered into the following M/WBE Small Purchase Method procurement with Statcare Urgent & Walk-In Medical for pre-employment medical testing services for Taxi and Limousine Inspector candidates. Contract No. CT1 156 20248804624: Contract Term 12/8/2023 12/31/2027 Contract Amount $399,160.00 TLC and the C

Contractor Address: 12975 Worldgate Drive, Suite 7322, Herndon, VA 20170-6008 Scope of Services: Emergency Medical Service (EMS) Computer Aided Dispatch (CAD) System Maintenance Maximum Value: $10,625,538.16 Term: 12/9/2025 through 12/8/2027 E-PIN: 05726S0004001 Procurement Method: Sole Source Procurement Policy Board Rule: Section 3-05 How can I comment on this proposed contract award? Please submit your comment to Fire Department o

item.title: Health and Mental Hygiene — Public Hearings and Meetings: NYC BOARD OF HEALTH MEETING ANNOUNCEMENT
item.description:
Outcome: Public Hearings and Meetings
NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that the Board of Health will hold a meeting on Tuesday, May 19, 2026 at 10:00 A.M. The meeting will be held at Gotham Center, 42-09 28 th Street, 8th Floor, Room 8-25, in Long Island City, NY. This meeting will be live-streamed and recorded, it can be viewed on the NYC DOHMH YouTube channel: https://www.youtube.com/nychealth. Documents and/or records from the meeting will be posted on the NYC DOHMH s webpage at https://www.nyc.gov/site/doh/about/hearings-and-notices/official-notices.page

item.title: Information Technology and Telecommunications — Public Comment on Contract Awards: 7-858-0801A - MWBE NG911 NYPD CAD Technical Project Manager - PM3
item.description:
Outcome: Public Comment on Contract Awards
This notice from NYC Office of Technology and Innovation is to seek public comments on the proposed contract detailed below. Contract Type: CT1 Contractor: Peer Consulting Resources, Inc. Contractor Address : 20 Jefferson Plaza, Princeton, NJ 08540 Scope of Services: NYPD CAD Technical PM Ensure the NG9-1-1 System integration and the existing configuration platforms. Manage a project team involved in the testing and implementation of the NG9-1-1 program. Ensure compliance with technical requirements and test management for the project. Liaison to agencies for project management of Class 1, Cla

item.title: Youth and Community Development — Procurement: Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP) NAQ
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP) providers through a variety of program models, serves youth age 14-24 thoughout New York City by providing opportunities to become familar with the world of work, gain employment experience, and identify edicational pathways that support career and lofe goals. This program is integral to the City's workforce development strategy and provides a critical intervention point for underserved youth population.

Maximum Va

item.title: Environmental Protection — Procurement: WFF-BT3-DESIGN Preliminary Design, DES, and DSDC for the Rondout West Branch Bypass Tunnel Connection
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
The Department of Environmental Protection is entering into a Negotiated Acquisition (NA) contract with Delve Underground Engineering PLLC (Delve). This NA will allow the agency to retain the current contractor to continue providing support and engineering for this complex and critical infrastructure WFF-BT-#-DES is for Preliminary Design, Design, and Design Services During Construction for the Rondout West Branch Bypass Tunnel Connection The contract term will be from 6/30/2026-11/4/2036 EPIN: 82626N0007 Vendors that are interested in expressing interest in similar procurements in the future

Contractor Address: 36 East 12th Street, 5th Floor, New York, NY 10003 Scope of Services: For the provision of providing maintenance and support of the Awards Electronic Health Record System. Maximum Value: $111,355.23 Term: 07/01/2025- 06/30/2028 Renewal Clauses : Not applicable E-PIN: 80626S0001001 Procurement Method: Sole Source Procurement Policy Board Rule: Sectio

item.title: Parks and Recreation — Procurement: Notice of Award for License Agreement No# B406-5-M ( License ) The Renovation, Operation and Maintenance of Paerdegat Squadron Marina at the Paerdegat Basin, Brookl
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
Permit No.: B406-5-M Permittee: Paerdegat Squadron, Inc. The City of New York Department of Parks & Recreation has awarded a concession to Paerdegat Squadron, Inc., whose address is 1330 Paerdegat Avenue North, Brooklyn, NY, 11236, for the renovation, operation and maintenance of Paerdegat Marina at the Paerdegat Basin in Brooklyn, NY. The concession, which was solicited by a Request for Proposals (RFP), will operate pursuant to a license agreement for one (1) twenty (20) year term, commencing upon written Notice to Proceed. The concessionaire shall pay to the City a guaranteed minimum annual

item.title: Information Technology and Telecommunications — Public Comment on Contract Awards: 7-858-0813A - MWBE NYC311 QA Functional Tester - Analyst 3
item.description:
Outcome: Public Comment on Contract Awards
This notice from NYC Office of Technology and Innovation is to seek public comments on the proposed contract detailed below. Contract Type: CT1 Contractor: Jedi Associates, Inc. Contractor Address : 106 Yarrow Circle, Dayton, NJ 08810 Scope of Services: The QA Functional Tester will be responsible for testing modifications to the 311 CSMS because of enhancements, defect fixes, platform upgrades or software maintenance among other things. Responsibilities include reviewing requirements for completeness and testability; preparing and coordinating test plans; developing and recording test cases;

item.title: Office of the Mayor — Special Materials: Emergency Executive Order No. 1.24
item.description: Outcome: Special Materials

item.title: Dept. of Social Svcs/Human Resources Administration — Procurement: Ingersoll Rand Rotary Screw Compressor and Service Agreement
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
Pursuant to Section 3-05 of the PPB Rules, the Department of Social Services Human Resources Administration (DSS/HRA) intends to enter into a Sole Source contract with Ingersoll Rand for Purchase of new IR RSA15I-138-TAS Compressor and Extended and Warranty/Service Agreement for the service period of 7/01/2026 - 6/30/2027. The HRA/GSS Bureau of Printing and Mail Distribution Center prints and distributes more than 12 million client benefit-related letters and over 4.5 million client benefit checks. These outputs are essential to ensuring that the City s most vulnerable residents receive timely

item.title: Office of the Mayor — Special Materials: Emergency Executive Order No. 2.23
item.description: Outcome: Special Materials

item.title: Information Technology and Telecommunications — Public Comment on Contract Awards: 7-858-0812A - MWBE NYC311 Project Manager - PM3
item.description:
Outcome: Public Comment on Contract Awards
This notice from NYC Office of Technology and Innovation is to seek public comments on the proposed contract detailed below. Contract Type: CT1 Contractor: Abrahams Consulting, LLC. Contractor Address : P.O. Box 10266, Staten Island, NY 10301 Scope of Services: The NYC311 Project Manager will assist with planning and coordinating team activities for the 311 CSMS. They will provide overall project management for different workstreams and serve as the first point of escalation for issues and risk management. They will assist with Program Intake, work planning efforts, Release Management and Supp

item.title: Homeless Services — Procurement: Midway Living Inc Services at FWC Ditmars Q 2025
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
The Department of Homeless Services works to prevent homelessness before it occurs, address street homelessness, and assist New Yorkers in transitioning from shelter and street homelessness to permanent housing. DHS collaborates with non-profit partners to provide temporary shelter and services that homeless New Yorkers need to achieve and maintain housing permanency. In addition to locating viable housing, the Tier II residence program stabilizes the family and promotes the move to independent living. Additionally, Non-Tier II certified residences would be expected to provide the same service

item.title: Economic Development Corporation — Procurement: Maritime Operators, Electric Barge Service Between Brooklyn Marine Terminal and Hunts Point Marine Terminal
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
New York City Economic Development Corporation (NYCEDC) invites expressions of interest from maritime operators, vessel designers, logistics firms, and technology partners for the development of an all-electric and/or zero-emissions (ZE) container-on-barge and associated tug service between the Brooklyn Marine Terminal (BMT) and the future Hunts Point Marine Terminal (HPMT), as well as the potential for future expansion to other strategic locations in New York Harbor and along the East Coast. This service represents a strategic investment in improving food resiliency, strengthening redundancy

item.title: Youth and Community Development — Procurement: Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP) Continuation Plan Contract Consolidation for Community
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP) providers through a variety of program models, serves youth ages 14-24 throughout New York City by providing opportunities to become familiar with the world of work, gain employment experience, and identify educational pathways that support career and life goals. This program is integral to the City s workforce development strategy and provides a critical intervention point for underserved youth population.

item.description:
Outcome: Public Comment on Contract Awards
This is a notice that Department of Environmental Protection Is seeking comments from the public about the proposed contract below. Contract Type: Contract Contractor: Arcadis-AKRF JV for CERQCTS Contractor Address: The Woolworth Building 233 Broadway, 16 th Floor, New York, NY 10007 Scope of Service: CEQR Compliance and Technical Support Maximum Value: $ 5,000,000.00 Term: The Contract term shall be 1,825 consecutive calendar days from the date of the written notice to proceed Renewal Clauses: Not Applicable E-Pin: 82626P0006 Procurement Method: Competitive Sealed Proposal Procurement Policy

item.title: Public Design Commission — Public Hearings and Meetings: Design Commission Meeting Agenda, Monday, May 18, 2026
item.description:
Outcome: Public Hearings and Meetings
Design Commission Meeting Agenda Monday, May 18, 2026 Meeting Location: Public Design Commission meetings are being held in-person at the Public Design Commission Board Room on the 3rd floor of City Hall. Members of the public can attend and give testimony either in-person or remotely. The entrance to City Hall, restrooms, and the boardroom are wheelchair accessible. To attend or testify remotely, the public can join the meeting via Microsoft Teams at https://teams.microsoft.com/meet/242262202724731?p=f4d3uPruZVFmW7kVEs or by calling +1 332-910-6701 and using the phone conference ID: 687 450 5

item.title: Design and Construction — Public Comment on Contract Awards: PWDNVIDE1, Purchase of IT Equipment
item.description:
Outcome: Public Comment on Contract Awards
This is a notice that NYC Department of Design and Construction is seeking comments from the public about the proposed contract below. Contract Type: Contract Contractor: Ohel Children's Home and Family Services Inc Contractor Address: 1268 East 14th Street, Brooklyn, NY, 11230 Project ID: PWDNVIDE1 Scope of Services: Discretionary Contract for the Purchase of IT Equipment for Ohel Children s Home and Family Services, Inc. Maximum Value: $749,000.00 Term: The contract term shall be 5 years from the date of registration E-PIN: 85026L0029001 Procurement Method: Line-Item Appropriation/Discretion
